text: After root cannulation, the ascending aorta was cross clamped with a detachable globular clamp.
Cardioplasia was achieved through root infusion of the del nido solution.
The mitral valve was then carefully examined.
and successfully repaired with ACC time 64 minutes as planned.
The left atriotomy was next repaired, and the aortic cross clamp was released.
Sudden aortic swelling due to adventitial hematoma occurring along the ascending aorta was immediately observed.
The presence of acute aortic dissection was confirmed on interoperative transesophageal echocardiogram.
A large, sheared-off intimal flap was noted.
This shows the moment of dissection development at the clamp site and the immediate growth adventitial hematoma extending towards the aortic root.
Too much pressure on the cross clamp may have caused the intimal injury.
After placing aortic purse strings and cannulating the ascending aorta, an adventitial hematoma spreading rapidly towards the aortic root was immediately observed.
A new type A aortic dissection was confirmed on interoperative echocardiogram.
The aortic cannula was quickly repositioned by advancing it deeper into the aortic trillumen.
The body temperature was lowered to 25°C following by cable cannulation and LV venting.
The dissection extended proximally to the aortic root and involved the right coronary ostium as well.
The surgical plan was now changed to a bental procedure.
Open distal anastomosis was performed under deep hypothermic circulatory arrest at 25 degrees Celsius.
The anastomosis site was reinforced with Teflon felt strips.
After completing the distal anastomosis and restoring systemic perfusion through the graft site arm, the bental procedure was commenced.
The dissected and friable RCA button was reinforced with a donut-shaped Teflon felt strip.
The distal and proximal graft-to-graft anastomosis was then performed.
And the urgent and unexpected bental hemiarch replacement surgery was successfully completed.
